name: Robert Morris
role: Founding Pastor, Gateway Church, Southlake, Texas
incident: Robert Morris, founding pastor of Gateway Church and former spiritual adviser to President Trump, pleaded guilty on October 2, 2025 to five counts of lewd and indecent acts with a child in Oklahoma. The abuse began in 1982 when the victim was 12 years old. Morris had previously served on Trump's evangelical advisory board in 2016 and was tapped by Governor Greg Abbott in 2017 to support the Bathroom Bill, arguing it would protect children from sexual abuse.

name: Robert Morris
role: Founding Pastor, Gateway Church, Southlake, Texas
incident: Robert Morris was indicted in Oklahoma on March 12, 2025 on five counts of lewd or indecent acts with a child. The indictment came after victim Cindy Clemishire publicly disclosed that Morris had abused her beginning at age 12 in 1982 when he was a traveling evangelist staying with her family. Morris had publicly advocated for the 2017 Texas Bathroom Bill by arguing it was needed to protect children from sexual abuse.

name: Mitchell Summerfield
role: Pastor, Word of God Fellowship Church, Raleigh, North Carolina
incident: Mitchell Summerfield, pastor of the Word of God Fellowship Church in Raleigh, pleaded guilty on May 27, 2025 to conspiracy to commit bank fraud and wire fraud. Between July 2020 and July 2021, Summerfield submitted false and fraudulent applications for PPP loans and Economic Injury Disaster loans totaling more than $400,000 across multiple entities he controlled. He faces a maximum sentence of 30 years in prison.

name: Paul Mitchell
role: Lead Pastor and Daycare President, Brooklyn, New York
incident: Paul Mitchell, lead pastor of a Brooklyn church and president of a church-affiliated daycare, pleaded guilty on December 16, 2025 to tax evasion. Between 2015 and 2022, Mitchell used his organizations' credit cards to pay for personal expenses including men's clothing, thousands of dollars in jewelry, luxury watches, and life insurance premiums. U.S. Attorney Joseph Nocella stated Mitchell treated his organizations' accounts as a personal piggy bank, stealing millions and betraying his congregation.

name: John McKinzie
role: Lead Pastor, Hope Fellowship Church, Frisco, Texas
incident: John McKinzie, 59, lead pastor of Hope Fellowship Church in Frisco, Texas, resigned on September 14, 2025 after confessing to 'sexual sin and moral failure' during a meeting with church leadership. He was the second pastor at the same megachurch to step down over similar circumstances in 2025. Church elders stated no one on staff had any prior knowledge of the moral failure before McKinzie disclosed it.

name: Rony Denis
role: Leader, House of Prayer Christian Churches, Hinesville, Georgia
incident: On September 10, 2025, a federal court unsealed a 26-count indictment against eight members of the House of Prayer Christian Churches in Operation False Profit. Rony Denis and associates were charged with conspiracy to commit bank fraud, wire fraud, and filing false tax returns. The indictment alleged defendants fraudulently received VA education benefits intended for military veterans while enriching themselves at church members' expense.

name: Brady Boyd
role: Senior Pastor, New Life Church, Colorado Springs, Colorado
sourceLabel: Juicy Ecumenism / Institute on Religion and Democracy
incident: Brady Boyd, senior pastor of New Life Church in Colorado Springs, stepped down in June 2025 after court documents in the Robert Morris Gateway Church case revealed that Boyd had known of Morris's prior misconduct for years, including while Boyd was being interviewed by New Life Church in 2007. Church officials determined Boyd had shared inaccurate information with the congregation and was not fully trustworthy before the congregation.

name: Dillon Awes
role: Pastor, Stedfast Baptist Church, Watauga, Texas
incident: Pastor Dillon Awes of Stedfast Baptist Church, designated an anti-LGBT hate group by the Southern Poverty Law Center, delivered a sermon calling for gay people to be charged with crimes, convicted, sentenced to death, and shot in the back of the head. Awes has repeated this call in multiple documented sermons across multiple years. The church's lead pastor was subsequently revealed to have solicited prostitutes and engaged in gambling, forcing the church to split.

name: Jonathan Shelley
role: Lead Pastor, Stedfast Baptist Church, Watauga, Texas
incident: Jonathan Shelley, lead pastor of Stedfast Baptist Church, told an Arlington City Council meeting that God has ruled homosexuality is a crime worthy of capital punishment. Shelley's church website explicitly states the government should impose the death penalty for homosexuality. Shelley's own documented personal conduct, which involved soliciting prostitutes and gambling, subsequently forced the church to split.

name: Kenneth Copeland
role: Founder and Senior Pastor, Eagle Mountain International Church, Newark, Texas
incident: Kenneth Copeland, one of the wealthiest televangelists in America with a reported net worth exceeding $760 million and three private jets paid for by ministry funds, conducted a televised ceremony on April 5, 2020 in which he claimed to blow the wind of God through the television screen to destroy COVID-19. Copeland has publicly stated that flying commercial is flying in a tube with a bunch of demons, then asked followers to fund his private jet fleet through donations.

name: Jesse Duplantis
role: Founder, Jesse Duplantis Ministries, Destrehan, Louisiana
incident: Jesse Duplantis, a Louisiana televangelist, broadcast a video on May 28, 2018 asking followers to fund a $54 million Dassault Falcon 7X private jet for his ministry. Duplantis argued that Jesus would not ride a donkey today and that having a private jet allows him to spread the gospel faster. Duplantis already owned three private jets at the time of the fundraising request.
